  • Method Details

    • id

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder id(@Nullable String id)
      The Media is assigned a universal unique identifier, which can contain a maximum of 255 characters.

    • trial

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder trial(@Nullable Boolean trial)
      FastPix allows for a free trial. Create as many media files as you like during the trial period. Remember, each clip can only be 10 seconds long and will be deleted after 24 hours.

      Also, all trial content will have the FastPix logo watermark.

    • status

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder status(@Nullable CreateMediaResponseStatus status)
      Determines the media's status, which can be one of the possible values.

    • createdAt

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder createdAt(@Nullable OffsetDateTime createdAt)
      Time the media was created, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).

    • updatedAt

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder updatedAt(@Nullable OffsetDateTime updatedAt)
      Time the media was updated, defined as a localDateTime (UTC Time).

    • playbackIds

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder playbackIds(@Nullable List<PlaybackId> playbackIds)
      A collection of Playback ID objects utilized for crafting HLS playback URLs.

    • metadata

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder metadata(@Nullable Map<String,String> metadata)
      You can search for videos with specific key value pairs using metadata, when you tag a video in "key" : "value" pairs. Dynamic metadata allows you to define a key that allows any value pair. You can have maximum of 255 characters and upto 10 entries are allowed.

    • mediaQuality

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder mediaQuality(@Nullable CreateMediaResponseMediaQuality mediaQuality)
      The quality tier applied to the media.

    • sourceAccess

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder sourceAccess(@Nullable Boolean sourceAccess)
      The sourceAccess parameter determines whether the original media file is accessible. Set to true to enable access or false to restrict it.

    • maxResolution

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder maxResolution(@Nullable CreateMediaResponseMaxResolution maxResolution)
      The maximum resolution tier defines the highest quality at which your media is available.

    • inputs

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder inputs(@Nullable List<CreateMediaResponseInput> inputs)
      A list of media input sources to be processed.

    • optimizeAudio

      public CreateMediaResponse.Builder optimizeAudio(@Nullable Boolean optimizeAudio)
      Enhance the quality and volume of the audio track. This is available for pre-recorded content only.

    • build

      public CreateMediaResponse build()
